items.title: Privatise your Data Streams with Bring Your Own Cloud (BYOC)
items.description:
TL;DR

Traditional SaaS streaming requires exporting sensitive data to a vendor cloud, creating security risks and egress costs. BYOC reverses this model by running the data plane inside the customer’s cloud while the vendor manages the control plane. This keeps data within the enterpri

items.title: Fix Docker Exit Code 137 (OOMKilled): Why It Happens and How to Stop It
items.description: Your container died and docker ps -a shows something like Exited (137) 4 minutes ago. Nine times out of ten that's the kernel's OOM killer, not your app crashing on its own. Here's what exit code 137 actually means and how I go about stopping it from happening again.

items.title: The Problem: The "Extract-Edit-Recompress" Loop
items.description:
We've all been there. You have a compressed file, you need to change one tiny thing inside it, so you:

Extract the whole thing.
Find the file and edit it.
Delete the old archive.
Re-compress the folder.
It’s a waste of time and storage. I wanted something that felt more lik

items.title: translateZ()
items.description: The translateZ() function moves an element closer to or farther from the user. translateZ() originally handwritten and published with love on CSS-Tricks. You should really get the newsletter as well.

items.title: translateY()
items.description: The translateY() function shifts an element vertically by the specified amount. translateY() originally handwritten and published with love on CSS-Tricks. You should really get the newsletter as well.

items.title: translateX()
